label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's think through this step by step. First, you take 7 steps forward. Let's say I'm facing north and take 7 steps north. Now I'm at position +7 (assuming each step is 1 unit in the north direction). Then I take 8 steps. Continuing in the same direction (north), so now I'm at +7 + 8 = +15. Then I take 10 steps. Still in north direction, so I'm at +15 + 10 = +25. Then I turn around. Now I'm facing south. Then I turn around again. Now I'm back to facing north. Then I take 5 steps. Since I'm facing north again, I go from +25 to +25 + 5 = +30. Then I turn around. Now I'm facing south. So I'm at position +30 and facing south. The question is, did I return to the starting point? The starting point was 0. I'm at +30, so I did not return to the starting point. Wait, let me double check that. Did I interpret the instructions correctly? 1. Take 10 steps (in the same forward direction) 4. Turn around (now facing opposite direction) 5. Turn around (now back to original direction) 6. Take 5 steps (in the original direction) 7. Turn around (now facing opposite direction again) So the net movement is 7 + 8 + 10 + 5 = 30 steps in the original direction. After all these steps, I'm at position +30, facing in the opposite direction from my final position. So I did not return to the starting point.
